name: ESP32 Dual-Band Wardriver
label: ESP32 Dual-Band Wardriver (justcallmekoko) — passive WiGLE logging
description: justcallmekoko/ESP32DualBandWardriver — a passive dual-band (2.4 + 5 GHz) Wi-Fi and BLE wardriver for the ESP32-C5. Scans and logs nearby networks/devices to an SD card in WiGLE CSV format for upload to wigle.net. Passive receive only — no transmit, deauth, or injection. Cyber Controller flashes the pinned, SHA-256-verified release images and adds no capability of its own.

firmware_urls.note: ESP-IDF project; each release ships bootloader.bin + partitions.bin + a date-stamped app bin (c5_wardriver_<ver>_<date>_c5_devkit.bin). Pinned + SHA-256-verified at flash time, never vendored.

assets.name: c5_wardriver_v2_3_0_20260709_c5_devkit.bin
assets.label: ESP32 Dual-Band Wardriver app image (passive WiGLE logging)

note: Assets live-verified via gh 2026-07-10 (tag v2.3.0, latest, prerelease:false): bootloader.bin (20464 B), partitions.bin (3072 B), c5_wardriver_v2_3_0_20260709_c5_devkit.bin (1820656 B). SHA-256 digests are REAL — computed from the downloaded v2.3.0 assets (bl 3e2b92a7… / part 0a8b5720… / app 8a009559…), so verify_sha256 passes against the pinned tag. Offsets are the known-correct ESP32-C5 values: bootloader 0x2000 (the central C5 gotcha — matches _bootloader_offset('esp32c5')), partitions 0x8000, app 0x10000, single-app layout (NO boot_app0). LICENSE: repo now ships an MIT LICENSE (the earlier 'no license' note is superseded); CC still only fetches the upstream release at flash time and never vendors the bins. SD card required for logging (Solo/Core mode). Offsets kept treat-as-verify: until a real ESP32-C5 flash confirms boot (Phase-F HW gate); the SHAs are final.
